div1.entryFree: Shuffle, to practise shifts, to play tricks: I am fain to s., to hedge and to lurch, Wiv. II, 2, 25. 'tis like the forced gait of a --ing nag, H4A III, 1, 135 (viz the gait of 'mincing poetry'). 'tis not so above; there is no --ing, Hml. III, 3, 61. with a little -- ing you may choose a sword unbated, IV, 7, 138. your life must s. for itself, Cymb. V, 5, 105. With an accus. denoting the effect: he shall likewise s. her away, Wiv. IV, 6, 29. oft good turns are --d off with such uncurrent pay (thanks) Tw. III, 3, 16 (== are eluded, got rid of). when we have --d off this mortal coil, Hml. III, 1, 67 (have got rid, in any way, of this troublesome life).
